headline: What Could Push BSX Stock Higher From Here?
summary: While a guidance cut has reset expectations, several core Boston Scientific franchises are accelerating. The Neuromodulation and Interventional Oncology businesses both posted organic sales growth of 15% last quarter. This internal momentum in high-value categories contrasts sharply with the new, more cautious company-wide outlook.

raw.headline: What Could Push BSX Stock Higher From Here?
raw.summary: While a guidance cut has reset expectations, several core Boston Scientific franchises are accelerating. This internal momentum in high-value categories contrasts sharply with the new, more cautious company-wide outlook.

headline: This ‘Dividend’ ETF Pays Just 1.2% | So Why Do Serious Investors Keep Buying It?
summary: The WisdomTree U.S. Quality Dividend Growth Fund (NASDAQ:DGRW) pays a trailing yield of roughly 1.28%, which sounds thin for something with “Dividend” in its name and downright embarrassing next to the 3%-plus yields on traditional income ETFs. And yet DGRW keeps pulling in serious institutional capital. PNC, Bank of America, and Ameriprise all lifted their ... headline: The Portfolio That Could Put You in a New Car Every Year for Life
summary: The average new vehicle in the United States now costs roughly $49,000, with full-size pickups and many luxury models pushing far higher. That puts a quietly absurd idea within reach for people who think in terms of dividend income: building a portfolio that throws off enough cash every year to buy a new car without ... headline: What Would It Take to Give Away 10% of Your Income Every Year?
summary: Most spending benefits the person doing the spending. Charitable giving is different. People give because they believe in a cause, want to help others, improve their community, honor a loved one, or simply because generosity brings satisfaction. Many religious traditions encourage giving as well. In the Judeo-Christian tradition, a 10% tithe has been a longstanding ... What Would It Take to Give Away 10% of Your Income Every Year?

headline: $500 a Month in These Stocks Will Generate $6,700 in Passive Annual Income

headline: The Portfolio That Pays For Your Bucket List
summary: Most bucket-list goals come with a price tag. Taking classes, hosting a family reunion, funding a scholarship for a grandchild, visiting your ancestors’ hometown in the Old Country, or finally seeing the Northern Lights all require money. Whatever form they take, the challenge is the same: how do you fund meaningful goals and experiences while ... The Portfolio That Pays For Your Bucket List

headline: Is It Worth Investing in Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Based on Wall Street's Bullish Views?
summary: According to the average brokerage recommendation (ABR), one should invest in Johnson & Johnson (JNJ). It is debatable whether this highly sought-after metric is effective because Wall Street analysts' recommendations tend to be overly optimistic. Would it be worth investing in the stock?

headline: Here’s What It Costs to Buy Back Your Fridays
summary: Most retirement calculators ask the wrong question. They assume the only goal is to stop working completely. Many workers would happily settle for something smaller: a three-day weekend, every week. For a worker earning roughly $80,000 a year, Fridays off are cumulatively worth about $16,000 annually. Replace that income and a five-day workweek becomes a ... Here’s What It Costs to Buy Back Your Fridays

headline: 3 Dividend Stocks to Buy Hand Over Fist in July

The thesis is simple. When defensive cash-flow machines start outperforming, ... 3 Dividend Stocks to Buy Hand Over Fist in July

headline: Here’s How Much You Need to Replace $70,000 in Salary With Dividend Income
summary: Replacing a $70,000 salary with dividend income comes down to one variable: yield. At a 3% blended yield you need roughly $2.33 million invested. At a 10% blended yield, you need roughly $700,000. Same paycheck, very different portfolios, very different risk profiles. Here is how the math breaks at three tiers, using real stocks with ... Here’s How Much You Need to Replace $70,000 in Salary With Dividend Income
